title: Add barcode width settings to Label Settings tab

requirements.summary: Add barcode width input field to Label Settings tab UI with validation, store persistence, live preview, and CPCL generation support
acceptanceCriteria: A barcode width input control is visible in the Label Settings tab alongside other barcode layout settings.
Changing the barcode width value updates the labelSettingsStore state.
The barcode width value persists across page reloads via localStorage.
The LabelPreview component reflects the updated barcode width in real time.
Invalid barcode width values are caught by barcodeSettingsValidation and display a user-friendly error message.
The barcode width setting is included in label settings import/export (JSON serialization).
The CPCL generator uses the stored barcode width value when generating print commands.

file_operations.description: Add barcodeWidth field (bigint) to the LabelSettings type and state object in the labelSettingsStore. Initialize with a default value consistent with existing dot-based units (e.g., 2n for standard barcode width). Ensure BigInt serialization handles the new field during localStorage persistence and rehydration.

Add a barcode width input field to the Label Settings tab UI. Place it alongside existing barcode layout controls (near barcodeHeight). Wire the input to read from and write to labelSettingsStore.barcodeWidth. Include appropriate label, input validation, and error display. Trigger validation via barcodeSettingsValidation when the value changes.

Extend the validation logic to include barcode width validation. Check that barcodeWidth is a positive number within reasonable bounds (e.g., 1-10 dots for standard barcodes). Return user-friendly error messages for invalid barcode width values (e.g., 'Barcode width must be between 1 and 10 dots').

Update the LabelPreview component to consume the barcodeWidth value from labelSettingsStore. Pass the barcode width to the barcode preview generation logic to adjust the visual representation of barcode bars in the preview. Ensure the preview reflects barcode width changes in real time.

Modify the barcode SVG generation function to accept a width parameter (in dots). Use the width value to scale the bar widths in the generated SVG pattern, ensuring the barcode preview accurately reflects the configured barcode width setting.

Update the CPCL command generation logic to include the barcode width value from labelSettingsStore when generating barcode print commands. Ensure the CPCL barcode command uses the stored barcodeWidth value (converted to the appropriate CPCL parameter format) alongside existing barcode type and height settings.

Ensure the import/export serialization functions handle the barcodeWidth field. Include barcodeWidth in JSON serialization/deserialization with BigInt support. Add migration logic to handle legacy settings without barcodeWidth by applying the default value.
